subject: Set aai&music Cassandra readiness periodSeconds
commit_message:
Set aai&music Cassandra readiness periodSeconds

Change-Id: I585e126601dc423caba3fa2af9ca4c80de0bf699
Issue-ID: OOM-1795
Signed-off-by: Yang Xu <yang.xu3@huawei.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1955/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/1955

Patch Set 1:

Results comparing before and after the patch:

before the change:
root@sb01-rancher:~# kubectl -n onap describe pod dev-aai-aai-cassandra-0 | grep Readiness
   Readiness:      exec [/bin/bash -c nodetool status | grep $POD_IP | awk '$1!="UN" { exit 1; }'] delay=10s timeout=600s period=10s #success=1 #failure=3


after the change:
root@sb01-rancher:~# kubectl -n onap describe pod dev-aai-aai-cassandra-0 | grep Readiness
    Readiness:      exec [/bin/bash -c nodetool status | grep $POD_IP | awk '$1!="UN" { exit 1; }'] delay=10s timeout=1s period=600s #success=1 #failure=3

Patch Set 1:

Referring to https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-probes/#configure-probes

The new "delay=10s timeout=1s period=600s" also seems wrong due to 10 minute period between probes. It would be too slow to respond to changes. Also the timeout may now be too short on a heavily loaded system.

Maybe "delay=10s timeout=10s period=10s" is a nicer compromise?

Patch Set 1:

the nodetool status check is so abusive that Cassandra with no appliction takes a factor of 2x cpu cycles of any other container in the system. nodetool is simply too intense and should be changed to a simple tcp port check or something if you want to run it more than once every 10 or 15 minutes.

could it be related to https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CASSANDRA-14475 ?

Patch Set 1:

> could it be related to https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CASSANDRA-14475
 > ?

The patch is to fix the mismatch of attribute and its value in template. The actual value shown in the results is set from integration-override.yaml, which is part of Integration deployment parameter we exercise at deployment time. 

I have submitted the test results above, and the actual value of timeout is out of scope this patch. Please approve.
